title: Chimonobambusa angulata
intro: Chimonobambusa angulata, also known as Angle Bamboo, is a species of bamboo native to China and Southeast Asia, characterized by its distinctive angled culms. The plant has been traditionally used in medicine for its various health benefits, including anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial properties, which may help alleviate symptoms of fever, headache, and digestive issues. The bioactive constituents of Chimonobambusa angulata include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and polysaccharides, which contribute to its medicinal properties. In traditional herbal medicine, the plant is often prepared as a decoction or tea, and is also used to make infusions and tinctures, which are used to treat a range of health conditions.
taxonomy: Chimonobambusa angulata belongs to the family Poaceae, commonly known as the grass family. Within this family, it is classified under the subfamily Bambusoideae, which includes bamboo species. This specific species is further categorized under the tribe Bambuseae, a group of woody grasses. The genus Chimonobambusa is a type of temperate bamboo found in East Asia, and Chimonobambusa angulata is one of the species within this genus. Chimonobambusa angulata is native to China and is valued for its ornamental and landscaping uses.

parts: Chimonobambusa angulata, also known as Japanese golden bamboo, is a species of bamboo native to East Asia.
